meta_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve no marketing skills (Beginner Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no marketing skills. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Starter Kit to Solve No Marketing Skills
content.seo_description: Learn how to build a SaaS starter kit to overcome lack of marketing skills. A beginner-level guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch fast.
content.answer_block: Stop letting a lack of marketing skills paralyze your progress. By leveraging a SaaS starter kit, you automate the infrastructure, authentication, and payment logic, allowing you to focus purely on building product-market fit. This approach minimizes technical overhead, letting your code do the heavy lifting while you learn to ship.
content.intro:
The blank screen is a graveyard for potential. You have the technical drive to build, but the daunting shadow of 'marketing' makes every line of code feel futile. You aren't just a student; you are a builder trapped in a cycle of over-engineering, terrified that if you build it, no one will come.

But here is the secret: you don't need a marketing degree. You need a system. By using a SaaS starter kit, you transform your development workflow into a high-velocity engine. We are going to bypass the noise and use Antigravity and Claude Code to turn your vision into a live, scalable reality.

steps.title: Architect the Core Identity
steps.description: Define your SaaS value prop using a starter kit scaffold. We focus on the landing page and auth flow first to ensure users can actually sign up.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a Next.js SaaS starter kit with Supabase integration, focusing on a clean, conversion-optimized landing page and user authentication flow.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate the directory structure for a Next.js SaaS project with Auth and Stripe integration.'

steps.title: Implement Subscription Logic
steps.description: Marketing starts with revenue. Integrate Stripe to handle billing so you can validate your idea immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Setup Stripe webhooks and product pages in the starter kit to handle monthly recurring revenue for a SaaS application.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Add a Stripe checkout session handler and update the database schema for user subscription status.'

steps.title: Build the Automated Waitlist
steps.description: Capture emails even before the product is finished. This builds your marketing foundation while you code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a high-converting waitlist form that saves emails to a Supabase database and triggers an automated welcome email.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Create a server-side API route to handle form submissions and store emails in Supabase.'

steps.title: Deploy to Global Edge
steps.description: Performance is your first marketing tool. Deploying to Vercel/Edge ensures a lightning-fast experience.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure the deployment pipeline for Vercel, ensuring environment variables for Stripe and Supabase are correctly mapped.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate a vercel.json file and optimize the build script for production deployment.'

steps.title: Feedback Loop Integration
steps.description: Embed a simple feedback widget so your first users become your marketing team through direct input.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Integrate a lightweight feedback component that saves user comments directly to a dashboard for the developer.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Add a feedback component that sends user input to the Supabase feedback table.'

pro_tips: Prioritize 'Build in Public' by pushing your repo commits to a Twitter thread; it is the most effective marketing for beginners.
Use a single-page landing site for your first 30 days to avoid feature creep and focus on conversion metrics.
Automate your onboarding emails using Resend or Postmark immediately; don't rely on manual follow-ups.
Focus on one specific 'pain point' rather than a broad solution; specific niches are easier to market without skills.
Treat your codebase as a living document by adding 'README' sections that explain the 'Why' behind every feature.
content.next_steps: Once your starter kit is live, identify your first 10 beta users through niche subreddits or developer communities to validate your solution.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to stop dreaming and start shipping. Every line of code you write today is an asset that works for you tomorrow. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
